evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/definic.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official product site describes dashboard, assessment, intelligence, benchmarking, management, and marketplace workflows for enterprise IT vendor selection.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The product copy says Definic turns vague project ideas into structured RFPs, matches vendors using real delivery data, benchmarks market rates, and tracks delivery performance.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The official press announcement says Definic gives procurement teams visibility into vendor fit, project requirements, and live market pricing.

risks: Research risk candidate: Definic's strongest proof points come from early client examples, so the open question is whether savings and cycle-time reductions repeat across more sectors and geographies.
TODO: State the most important weekly evidence gap or product risk without guessing.
picks.verdict: TODO: One source-bound conclusion that a reader can evaluate from the evidence above.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/equal-ai.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official product site describes Equal AI as an AI call assistant that picks up, speaks, and screens unknown calls for the user.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The Google Play listing describes live call-screening transcripts, caller-intent summaries, one-tap joining, typed messages read by the assistant, full recordings, and post-call summaries.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: TechCrunch reports the app has more than one million monthly active users, more than 300,000 daily active users, and support for over 10 languages.

risks: Research risk candidate: The product still has to prove it can defend privacy expectations and keep call-handling quality high as it expands beyond unknown-number screening.
TODO: State the most important weekly evidence gap or product risk without guessing.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/phoenixai.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes PhoenixAI as an analytical database for agentic AI that answers live-data SQL queries in sub-second time.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The product surface lists Kafka, Iceberg, Delta Lake, SOC 2 controls, row-level security, audit logging, and data masking.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The official careers page confirms a static hiring surface without requiring VentureDex to scrape roles, counts, locations, or compensation.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether buyers treat agent-facing analytics as a new database category or as faster OLAP positioning.
TODO: State the most important weekly evidence gap or product risk without guessing.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/rivvun-ai.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site positions Rivvun as an agentic execution layer for detecting spend leakage, margin erosion, and revenue leakage.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: Rivvun describes Spend Assurance and Margin Defense agents for invoice assessment, rebates, pricing enforcement, and commitments.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The official product surface cites outcomes including $70M spend reduction identified and 300% ROI on royalty governance.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether autonomous corrections can work across inconsistent policies, supplier data, and enterprise approval rules.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/poetic.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site positions Poetic as software that learns business rules and follows them while handling end-to-end processes.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The product surface describes uploading procedures, learning from expert feedback, and recovering when systems change.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: Poetic publishes process metrics including 99% quality auto-decisioning, 100% process adherence, and 500,000 disputes handled.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is how much autonomous process execution regulated enterprises will approve near KYC, fraud, or dispute decisions.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/niteshift.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site positions Niteshift as a full-stack cloud for coding agents, with setup generated from repo docs, CI, Docker, secrets, and services.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The product surface says agents can verify browser, API, database, log, and eval-script evidence before attaching PR proof.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: Niteshift lists Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Pi as supported agent choices rather than tying users to one model workflow.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether engineering teams will grant long-running agents enough repo and runtime access to make the cloud model useful.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/leaf.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes Leaf as a data and intelligence platform for agribusiness marketing, product, R&D, and commercial teams.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: Leaf says it connects OEMs, imagery, weather, soil labs, and farm management systems, then standardizes and spatially allocates farm data.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding announcement says Leaf processes farm data covering 20% of global crop acres annually.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether Leaf can keep data rights, provider integrations, and agribusiness buyer needs aligned at global scale.
